module_signals.finding: SEC EDGAR direct fetch (rmd-20250630.htm FY2025 10-K) returned HTTP 403 on first attempt; per robustness rule (max 2 attempts/URL, no retry-loop), pivoted immediately to web-search cross-corroboration (search results directly quoting 10-K figures) + stocktitan.net structured Q3 FY2026 8-K summary + stockanalysis.com aggregated financials + official ResMed IR/newsroom press releases. Every load-bearing FY2025/Q3 FY2026 figure (revenue, margin, EPS, FCF, debt, cash) cross-checked across >=2 independent sources. Price $209.63 confirmed via 2 independent sources (stockanalysis.com + web-search-sourced Yahoo Finance), both dated to the same 2026-07-02 close -- exact match to frozen as_of_price input. 51 claims registered in claim_ledger.csv; 27 sources in source_register.md. Completeness ~65% -- key gaps: RMD-specific China segment revenue not disclosed (O4), AI-attributable revenue/ASP premium not quantified (O5), full earnings-call transcript not fetched (O7), a ~$179M total-debt figure discrepancy between two secondary sources (O2). Caps at DECISION_DRAFT, consistent with target.

RMD operates a coherent device-plus-consumables-annuity model (devices ~52% of FY25 revenue, masks/accessories ~36%) layered with a genuinely differentiated AI/digital-health data platform: AirView (34M+ patients, 31.5M+ connected devices, 24B+ nights of data) and Smart Comfort (FDA-cleared Dec 2025, the first FDA-cleared AI-enabled medical device of its kind, trained on 100M+ nights of data). Masks/accessories (the highest-quality recurring revenue line) is growing FASTER than devices (+15% vs +9%, Q3 FY26) -- a genuinely positive mix signal. Unlike GEHC's AI products (several still pending clearance), RMD's flagship AI product has actually achieved regulatory clearance. The central strategic debate (GLP-1 drugs as tailwind vs. headwind) is directly, transparently addressed by company-disclosed real-world data (1.7M and 2.1M patient cohort studies showing higher resupply/adherence and PAP-initiation rates for GLP-1+PAP combination patients), though this data answers a narrower retention/adherence question rather than fully resolving the new-patient-diagnosis-funnel question. Signal = +1 (mildly positive): the mechanism is coherent, evidenced in data-scale/regulatory terms, and the company has engaged the central risk with real data rather than avoiding it -- though monetization of the AI layer in dollar terms remains unproven, same gap as the GEHC exemplar.

Moat is GOOD-TO-STRONG, materially better evidenced than the GEHC comparison point. The mask/accessories consumables annuity is genuine, growing, and driving multi-year gross-margin expansion (55.78% FY23 -> 62.2% Q3 FY26). The AirView/Smart Comfort AI data platform is real, regulatory-cleared (not merely launched/pending), and built on an irreplicable data-scale asset (100M+ nights training data). The post-Philips-recall duopoly position (RMD ~48% share) remains intact, though it is a maturing rather than freshly-compounding tailwind. The balance sheet is a clean, growing net-cash position (unlike GEHC's rising net debt) -- a genuine moat-adjacent margin-of-safety input. The one genuine, unresolved gap is forward-looking (GLP-1 new-patient-funnel effect), not a currently-observed deterioration -- a materially different evidentiary state than GEHC's confirmed, already-realized China revenue decline. China/emerging-market competitive risk (BMC Medical, Jiangsu Yuyue) is structurally plausible by analogy to the confirmed GEHC/United Imaging pattern but is NOT evidenced as currently binding for RMD specifically (no RMD-specific China segment data found -- O4). Operator grade 4/5 (long-tenured, evidenced multi-lever execution: growth + margin expansion + deleveraging + shareholder returns simultaneously). Signal = +1: moat is real, currently strengthening (not eroding), with one genuine unresolved forward-looking question rather than multiple already-realized negative trends.

Financial trend is the OPPOSITE pattern from the GEHC exemplar: margin EXPANDED in every period evidenced (gross margin 55.78% FY23 -> 56.67% FY24 -> 59.36% FY25 -> 62.2% Q3 FY26; operating margin 26.80% -> 28.17% -> 32.75%). Net income grew faster than revenue (24.9% vs 10.4% CAGR, FY23-FY25) -- genuine operating leverage. FCF conversion is excellent: FCF exceeded net income in both FY2024 (1.28x) and FY2025 (1.19x) -- a materially higher-quality cash-conversion profile than GEHC's ~0.70x. Balance sheet flipped from net debt (~$1.35B, FY2023) to a growing net-cash position (~$0.82-1.0B by Q3 FY2026) while simultaneously funding $500M of 9M-FY26 buybacks, a raised dividend, and a $340M bolt-on acquisition (Noctrix) -- no leverage strain evidenced. Q3 FY2026 revenue missed consensus by ~1.4% (a modeling miss, not a growth-rate deterioration -- +11% reported growth is consistent with, not worse than, the prior-year trend). Signal = +1: financial trend is positive and improving across every metric and every period evidenced, a genuine and meaningful positive differentiator vs. the GEHC comparison point.

The dominant kill path is GLP-1-driven new-patient-diagnosis-funnel shrinkage -- a real, high-materiality, genuinely unresolved multi-year structural question that current evidence cannot settle in either direction (company data addresses a narrower, favorable-framed retention/adherence question, not the funnel-size question directly). This is NOT yet evidenced as realized in RMD's actual financial results (all trends are currently positive) -- a forward-looking probability question, unlike GEHC's already-realized China/PCS/margin-compression findings. A second, career-relevant kill path (China/emerging-market budget competition, analogous to the confirmed GEHC/United Imaging pattern) is structurally plausible but NOT evidenced as currently binding for RMD specifically (O4) -- a genuinely different evidentiary state than GEHC's confirmed equivalent. A diffuse reimbursement/Medicaid-policy risk exists but is sector-wide, not RMD-specific in magnitude. Signal = 0 (neutral): one real, unresolved, high-materiality forward risk exists, but it is not yet realized in the data, and the balance sheet provides genuine downside protection if it does partially materialize -- a more balanced risk profile than GEHC's compounding, already-realized risk registry.

At $209.63 (~$30.24B mkt cap, ~$29.42B EV using Q3 FY26 net cash ~$0.82B): EV/FY2025-est-EBITDA ~15.8x; trailing P/E 20.21x (internally consistent: TTM EPS $10.37 x 20.21 = $209.58, matching price almost exactly). Price sits at -28.7% off 52wk high ($293.81), +16.3% above 52wk low ($180.27) -- NOT a hug situation on either end, and unlike GEHC's low-hug (justified by real deterioration), RMD's below-high positioning appears to be a SENTIMENT/NARRATIVE-driven de-rating (GLP-1 fear + a modest consensus miss + CFO-transition/M&A-announcement noise) that is NOT matched by evidenced fundamental deterioration over the same window (margin expanded, revenue grew, balance sheet strengthened throughout). RMD trades at a materially richer multiple than GEHC (~15.8x vs ~9.7x EV/EBITDA) -- NOT a statistically cheap setup, reflecting a real business-quality premium. 3-scenario IRR: bear -15.1%(3yr)/-12.7%(5yr); base +6.0%(3yr)/+7.9%(5yr, just barely under the 8% hurdle); bull +18.4%(3yr)/+18.4%(5yr). Base case comes CLOSER to clearing the hurdle than any other dossier reviewed this cycle (GEHC's base was ~flat/negative) but still technically fails at both horizons. Signal = 0: price offers essentially zero, not negative but also not meaningfully positive, margin of safety in the base case -- meaning the investment decision hinges on a narrow judgment about how much probability weight to assign the GLP-1 bear case, not on an obvious price-based mispricing in either direction.

starter_zone: $190-195 (~9-10% pullback from $209.63) -- base case restores a genuine, if modest, margin of safety (~10% 5yr IRR) without requiring the bull-case catalysts to already be proven. NOTE: one of five IC panel souls (Howard Marks) dissents, arguing a SMALL starter position is defensible even at the current $209.63 price given the sentiment/fundamentals divergence -- see ic_panel.md for the full recorded dissent.
add_zone: First STARTER at $190-195 or on confirmed evidence. Add toward CORE only as: (a) 1-2 more quarters (Q4 FY2026 ~2026-08-06, then Q1 FY2027) show device-revenue growth rate holding steady or accelerating (directly tests the GLP-1 funnel question), (b) any credible AI-revenue/ASP-attribution disclosure emerges (closes O5), (c) a future refresh confirms RMD-specific China/EM data shows NO GEHC-style share erosion (closes O4). No addition above current price without at least one of these confirmations.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: Do not chase above ~$225-230 without a corresponding fundamentals upgrade -- pure multiple expansion from here would push the already-thin base-case margin further underwater. Reassess toward AVOID on any of: device-segment revenue growth decelerating below +3-4% organic for 2 consecutive quarters without explanation, confirmed China/EM share erosion analogous to GEHC's United Imaging finding, margin reversal back toward FY2023 levels for 2+ quarters, or the net-cash position reversing into net debt without a clearly value-accretive reason.
binding_constraint: PRICE -- business quality is good-to-strong and currently improving on every metric evidenced (margin expansion, FCF quality, balance-sheet strength, a regulatory-cleared AI product), meaningfully better evidenced than the GEHC comparison point at a similar completeness level. The single genuine unresolved question is the GLP-1 new-patient-diagnosis-funnel effect -- a real, high-materiality, forward-looking structural risk that current evidence (including the company's own data) cannot settle in either direction, though the adjacent retention/adherence question is favorably evidenced. The stock fell 33% peak-to-trough in 2026 on this narrative fear plus a modest consensus miss plus CFO-transition/M&A-announcement noise, WITHOUT a corresponding evidenced fundamental deterioration -- this looks like a sentiment-driven de-rating on a genuinely strong business, closer to the MSFT exemplar's 'capex panic on an otherwise strong print' pattern than to GEHC's 'price correctly tracks real deterioration' pattern. However, the CURRENT price already reflects a real quality premium (20.2x trailing P/E, ~15.8x EV/EBITDA) such that the base-case 5yr IRR (+7.9%) sits just barely under the 8% hurdle -- offering essentially zero margin of safety, not a bargain. This is NOT a 'business quality itself is ambiguous' case (GEHC) or a 'business quality and price both work, just finish the research' case (MSFT) -- it is 'business quality is genuinely strong, price is fair-to-slightly-rich, one real question remains open,' which produces the CLOSEST panel split (4-1, not unanimous) of any dossier in this batch, correctly reflecting a genuinely harder call than GEHC's clean unanimous WATCH.
kill_criteria: K-A GLP-1 funnel shrinkage confirmed: device-segment revenue growth decelerates below +3-4% organic for 2 consecutive quarters WITHOUT a credible alternative explanation -> treat the GLP-1 bear case as materializing, downgrade toward AVOID and re-underwrite all scenarios.

K-B China/EM competition confirmed: a future refresh obtaining RMD-specific China/emerging-market segment data showing a GEHC-style share-loss pattern to domestic budget competitors (BMC Medical, Jiangsu Yuyue) -> re-underwrite the moat/bottleneck findings and treat as a second, compounding structural risk.

K-C Margin reversal: gross or operating margin fails to hold near current levels (reverses back toward FY2023-era 55.78%/26.80%) for 2+ consecutive quarters -> the margin-expansion thesis (the key positive differentiator vs GEHC) would be falsified.
K-D Balance sheet deterioration: net cash position reverses into net debt without a clearly value-accretive reason -> re-assess the balance-sheet-cushion argument supporting WATCH-not-AVOID.
K-E Valuation discipline: price rises above ~$225-230 without a corresponding fundamentals upgrade (pure multiple expansion) -> no-chase, reaffirm WATCH or downgrade attractiveness further.
runner_dissent: The panel is 4/5 WATCH with a genuine, recorded dissent from Howard Marks toward STARTER(small) -- the closest split of any dossier in this batch. I want to flag this dissent as decision-useful rather than noise: Marks's reasoning (sentiment has diverged from fundamentals in a directionally favorable way, warranting a small first tranche) is internally consistent and legitimately different from the majority's insistence on a price-level cushion regardless of sentiment direction. Both are defensible risk-management philosophies applied to the SAME evidence base, and Nick's own risk tolerance/conviction in the digital-health-AI career thesis should determine which framework he weights more heavily for RMD specifically. Separately, I'd flag that RMD's evidence base this run is meaningfully stronger and more favorable than the GEHC comparison point on nearly every financial and moat axis (margin expansion vs compression, FCF quality, net cash vs rising net debt, a regulatory-cleared vs pending AI product, a 13-year vs 3.5-year CEO tenure) -- the fact that RMD still lands at WATCH (not STARTER) is entirely a function of the PRICE already reflecting that quality premium, not any doubt about the underlying business. The single most decision-useful number in this dossier is the base-case 5yr IRR of +7.9% -- just 10bps under the 8% hurdle -- meaning this is the most 'live,' most price-sensitive call of any dossier in the batch: a ~9-10% pullback (to ~$190-195) would very plausibly flip the panel majority to STARTER without requiring any new positive evidence at all, just the passage of the market's own volatility. I would prioritize watching the price level over waiting for new fundamental catalysts, given how close the current setup already is. On the career-relevant question: RMD's AI moat (Smart Comfort, AirView) is evidenced in genuinely more concrete, FDA-cleared terms than GEHC's equivalent, but the GLP-1 funnel question is a novel, medtech-specific risk category (not present in the imaging/China thesis) that deserves its own dedicated tracking framework independent of the China-competition lens -- these are two genuinely different risk vectors that happen to both affect RMD, and should not be conflated in future refreshes.
